steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: “Many works ... are ___” is an independent clause. After the blank: “his Elements ... is an extant work” is an independent clause. The blank must connect these two clauses.

steps.title: Apply the conjunctive adverb rule
steps.content:
”Lost” completes the first clause. “Conversely” is a conjunctive adverb joining two independent clauses. When a conjunctive adverb begins a new sentence, the pattern is: period before it, comma after it (IC. Conversely, IC).

The other options either fail to provide the required punctuation (option B) or incorrectly use “and conversely” (options A and D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
